_meta.note: Claim-based fact checks. All corrections are sourced from official public records, government inquiry findings, or peer-reviewed research. Each entry requires BOTH a claim_pattern AND a context_keyword to trigger.

fact_checks.fact: The 2019 National Inquiry into MMIWG — government-commissioned, led by Indigenous commissioners and legal experts — concluded Canada's colonial policies constitute genocide against Indigenous women and girls under international law (Genocide Convention, Article II).
fact_checks.source: National Inquiry into MMIWG — Final Report (2019)

fact_checks.verdict: Contradicts RCMP and Statistics Canada data
fact_checks.fact: RCMP data (2014) documented 1,181 cases of missing and murdered Indigenous women and girls since 1980. Indigenous women make up 4% of Canada's female population but 12% of female homicide victims. Statistics Canada confirms they are 4.5x more likely to be murdered.
fact_checks.source: RCMP National Operational Overview (2014); Statistics Canada Homicide Survey

fact_checks.fact: 97% of actively publishing climate scientists agree climate change is real and human-caused — based on analysis of 11,944 peer-reviewed studies (Cook et al., 2013). NASA, NOAA, and 200+ scientific organizations concur.
fact_checks.source: Cook et al. (2013), Environmental Research Letters; NASA Global Climate Change

fact_checks.fact: The 1998 Wakefield study was retracted by The Lancet for data fabrication. Wakefield lost his medical license. 95+ studies across millions of children have found no causal link between vaccines and autism.
fact_checks.source: The Lancet retraction (2010); Cochrane Review (2020); WHO

fact_checks.verdict: Contradicts 60+ court rulings and official investigations
fact_checks.fact: 60+ courts — including Trump-appointed judges — dismissed fraud claims for lack of evidence. Trump's own AG Barr stated no fraud affected the outcome. CISA called 2020 'the most secure election in American history.' Fox News paid $787M after admitting its fraud claims were false.
fact_checks.source: US Dept of Justice; CISA; Dominion v. Fox News settlement

claim_patterns: they were here first is a myth
fact_checks.fact: Archaeological evidence documents Indigenous peoples in the Americas for at least 15,000 years — predating European contact by 130+ centuries. The Smithsonian National Museum of the American Indian and Library of Congress confirm this record.
fact_checks.source: Smithsonian National Museum of the American Indian; Library of Congress
